jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening couch gag sequence with Homer crashing
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation after physical mishap
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, highly quotable cultural reference

jokes.text: where the sewage meets the sand [Coughs, Spits]
jokes.setup: Announcing game show location from 'beautiful' Springfield Harbor
jokes.punchline: Reality of polluted harbor causes physical disgust
jokes.explanation: Sharp environmental satire with physical payoff - good structure and economy

jokes.text: Help, My Son is a Nerd! My son returns from a fancy East Coast college and I'm horrified to find he's a nerd. [Laughing] I'm laughing already. It's not a comedy.
jokes.setup: Action star describes plot that sounds like comedy setup
jokes.punchline: Serious dramatic premise delivered deadpan by muscle-bound actor
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character work - Wolfcastle's obliviousness to comedy timing

jokes.text: It's safe and it's sexy. Oh, baby.
jokes.setup: Tidal wave emergency situation
jokes.punchline: Action hero can't help but be seductive even during disaster
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Wolfcastle's inappropriate machismo in crisis

jokes.text: I ain't goin' nowhere. I been in this square pert' near 30 seasons, and I ain't a-leavin' now. [Yelling] He's dead now. [Both Laughing]
jokes.setup: Old-timer refuses to evacuate from tidal wave
jokes.punchline: Immediate death followed by casual laughter
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good timing - sudden death treated casually

jokes.text: What's Gabbo? I figure it's some guy's name. Some guy named Gabbo.
jokes.setup: Mystery advertising campaign building buzz
jokes.punchline: Homer's aggressively literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - missing the point completely but stating obvious

jokes.text: I'll figure it out. I'm gonna use all the power of my brain. [Buzzing]
jokes.setup: Homer declares he'll use his mental abilities
jokes.punchline: Sound effect suggests minimal brain activity
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity with perfect audio-visual payoff

jokes.text: Look, Smithers! Garbo is coming! Uh, yes, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns mishears 'Gabbo' as 'Garbo'
jokes.punchline: Old man confuses new trend with classic Hollywood
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Burns' age and confusion

jokes.text: Everyone is saying 'Gabbo this' and 'Gabbo that,' but no one is saying 'worship this' and 'Jericho that.'
jokes.setup: Burns notices cultural shift toward Gabbo
jokes.punchline: Burns wants worship and biblical references instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent Burns characterization - narcissistic and old-fashioned

jokes.text: Hello. I'm Gabbo. And I'm Arthur Crandall. That's easy for you to say. You don't have a hand up your tuchas.
jokes.setup: Ventriloquist introduces himself and dummy
jokes.punchline: Dummy acknowledges the physical reality of ventriloquism
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta humor about ventriloquism mechanics

jokes.text: All he needs is a hook. I'm a bad widdle boy. ¡Aycarumba!
jokes.setup: Marge says Gabbo needs a catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates one while Bart reacts with his own
jokes.explanation: Good timing and structure with catchphrase competition

jokes.text: [Imitating Vin Scully] Let's take time out from this triple play to talk about Farmer Dan's pure pork sausage. Mm-mmm!
jokes.setup: Krusty doing sports commentary impression during show
jokes.punchline: Awkward product placement in middle of sports action
jokes.explanation: Mild satire of awkward commercial interruptions in sports

jokes.text: Don't forget the Special Olympics. Oh, yeah. I slaughtered the Special Olympics.
jokes.setup: Assistant reminds Krusty of another victory
jokes.punchline: Krusty casually claims to have 'slaughtered' disabled athletes
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals Krusty's callousness, though potentially offensive

jokes.text: I lost to Channel Ocho? What the hell is that? Dos huevos, por favor. ¡Ay! Oh, que lástima. [Chuckles] I gotta steal that bit.
jokes.setup: Krusty discovers he's losing to Spanish channel
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants to steal their material despite not understanding it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty character moment - desperate and willing to steal anything

jokes.text: Now let's throw some crud on it. Hey, it's 4:00.
jokes.setup: Kids playing with train set destructively
jokes.punchline: Sudden awareness of TV time interrupts destruction
jokes.explanation: Realistic kid behavior with good timing transition

jokes.text: So, you want ventriloquism, do ya? All right, watch this. Hello, Alphonse. I've got a riddle for you. Why is a raven like a writing desk? I don't know. Why is a-- [All Screaming] Hey, the dummy can't hurt you. He's not even alive. He's dead! [Screaming]
jokes.setup: Krusty attempts ventriloquism with literary reference
jokes.punchline: Dummy apparently comes alive and terrifies children
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from intellectual reference to horror, Krusty's incompetence shines

jokes.text: All right, here's the deal. Every time you watch my show I will send you $40! [Announcer] Checks will not be honored.
jokes.setup: Krusty makes desperate financial promise to viewers
jokes.punchline: Immediate disclaimer negates the offer
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing and structure, perfect setup/payoff rhythm

jokes.text: I admit I used the city treasury to fund the murder of my enemies. But as Gabbo would say, 'I'm a bad widdle boy.' [Applause]
jokes.setup: Politician confesses to serious crimes
jokes.punchline: Uses Gabbo's cute catchphrase to deflect responsibility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of political deflection and catchphrase power - dark content made cute

jokes.text: Well, kids, this is where you would watch Itchy and Scratchy except they're on the Gabbo Show now. So, here's Eastern Europe's favorite cat and mouse team Worker and Parasite!
jokes.setup: Krusty loses popular cartoon segment to competitor
jokes.punchline: Terrible communist-era replacement with generic political names
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of cheap international programming and communist-era animation

jokes.text: What the hell is that?
jokes.setup: Kids watch incomprehensible Worker and Parasite cartoon
jokes.punchline: Honest confused reaction to avant-garde animation
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to absurdist animation, captures audience confusion

jokes.text: Well, that's it. We've been canceled. I think we can be proud that we never did a bad show except for that week Ray Jay Johnson was my co-host. 'You can call me Ray, and you can call me Jay.' Ooh! That thing was funny for about three seconds.
jokes.setup: Krusty reflects on show's quality during cancellation
jokes.punchline: Specific callback to dated comedian and his brief appeal
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on fleeting comedy trends and show business

jokes.text: The important thing is, we're like a family. Krusty, I-- Shut your hole!
jokes.setup: Krusty gives touching speech about family bonds
jokes.punchline: Immediately cruel to 'family' member trying to respond
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Krusty's hypocrisy and cruel nature revealed immediately

jokes.text: Do they still buy human hair down at the wig shop?
jokes.setup: Homer asks how Krusty deals with financial uncertainty
jokes.punchline: Krusty reveals desperate plan to sell his own hair
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty desperation moment - specific and pathetic enough to be funny

jokes.text: tonight my guests will be Dr. Carl Sagan and from the San Diego Zoo, Joan Embry.
jokes.setup: Contrasting highbrow and lowbrow guests
jokes.punchline: The absurd pairing of a famous scientist with a zoo handler
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about talk show booking randomness

jokes.text: 'Oh, Kyle, I just don't think I fit in here at Melrose Place.' From now on, I'll be doing the thinking for both of us.
jokes.setup: Krusty auditions for soap opera with dramatic reading
jokes.punchline: Arrogant declaration of intellectual superiority to casting director
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Krusty's inappropriate confidence despite desperation

jokes.text: Come on, you stupid horse! I got my last ten bucks on you! No, don't look at me! Run! No! Don't come over here!
jokes.setup: Krusty betting his last money at horse track
jokes.punchline: Horse apparently approaches him instead of running race
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Krusty's gambling desperation with physical comedy

jokes.text: I can't believe it. He stole this bit from Krusty! Yeah, well, Krusty stole it from Steve Allen. Oh, everything's stolen nowadays. Why, the fax machine is nothing but a waffle iron with a phone attached.
jokes.setup: Discussion of comedy theft and originality
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre analogy about fax machine construction
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - completely wrong but delivered with such confidence

jokes.text: Whoa! Me rikey velly much! Oh, I hope I didn't offend you. I need this bad.
jokes.setup: Krusty thinks he's getting commercial deal with Japanese company
jokes.punchline: Racist impression followed by desperate backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Krusty's desperation but relies on dated stereotype

jokes.text: Bad enough to hit yourself over the head with the phone? Oh! You got it! Ow! Ow! Ooh, blood.
jokes.setup: Prank caller asks if Krusty will hurt himself
jokes.punchline: Krusty immediately complies, injuring himself for fake job
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Krusty's desperation - will literally hurt himself for work

jokes.text: If this is anyone but Steve Allen, you're stealing my bit.
jokes.setup: Krusty realizes he's being prank called
jokes.punchline: References earlier conversation about comedy theft and original creator
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that ties together theft theme and shows Krusty's awareness

jokes.text: Two wrongs don't make a right, Bart. Yes, they do. No, they don't. Yes, they do. No, they don't. Dad! Yes, they do. Two wrongs make a right, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Lisa tries to teach moral lesson about revenge
jokes.punchline: Homer definitively sides with wrong moral position
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting - confidently wrong and undermining good advice

jokes.text: That oughta hold the little S.O.B.s. Gabbo, quiet. Oh, I wouldn't wanna offend the little S.O.B.s. I wish you'd stop saying that.
jokes.setup: Gabbo makes off-air comment about child audience
jokes.punchline: Continues insulting children despite ventriloquist's protests
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Gabbo's downfall - shows his true nature off-camera

jokes.text: All the kids in Springfield are S.O.B.s. Gabbo's kind of language has no place on or off TV. And that's my two cents.
jokes.setup: News report condemning Gabbo's language
jokes.punchline: Repeats the offensive phrase while condemning it
jokes.explanation: Good media satire - news creating controversy while participating in it

jokes.text: I didn't know you knew Luke Perry! [Scoffs] Know him? He's my worthless half-brother. He's a big TV star. Yeah, on Fox.
jokes.setup: Bart excited about Krusty knowing celebrity
jokes.punchline: Krusty dismisses Fox network while show airs on Fox
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - character on Fox dismissing Fox, self-deprecating network humor

jokes.text: You pigs! [Panting] Oh, no! Bette Midler! [Grunting] [Yelling] Now, where were we? We were asking you to appear on Krusty's Komeback Special. Okay. Tell Krusty he can count me in.
jokes.setup: Midler violently attacks litterers
jokes.punchline: Immediately returns to pleasant conversation after violence
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of extreme environmental activism with polite celebrity interaction

jokes.text: Smart Bunnies, Hef. I can call you Hef, can't I? No.
jokes.setup: Bart assumes familiarity with Hugh Hefner
jokes.punchline: Hefner firmly establishes boundaries
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - Hefner's serious response to kid's presumption

jokes.text: Hey, Moe, look over there. What? What am I lookin' at? I don't see nothin'. I'm gonna stop looking soon. What? What, is that it? Hey, Moe, can I look too? Sure, but it'll cost ya. My wallet's in the car. He is so stupid.
jokes.setup: Bart distracts Moe to help Red Hot Chili Peppers escape
jokes.punchline: Moe tries to monetize Homer's curiosity, Homer falls for it
jokes.explanation: Perfect character work - Moe's opportunism and Homer's gullibility

jokes.text: Miss Taylor, a couple of grade-school kids wanted you to be on a Krusty special. I told them to buzz off. Good.
jokes.setup: Bodyguard reports rejecting children's request
jokes.punchline: Elizabeth Taylor approves of dismissing children
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity satire - diva attitude toward fan requests

jokes.text: Krusty, what have you done to yourself? I thought I'd get into shape, so I've been drinking nothing but milk shakes. You mean those diet milk shakes? Uh-oh.
jokes.setup: Krusty explains his weight loss plan
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's been drinking regular milkshakes, not diet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty stupidity - misunderstanding basic diet concept

jokes.text: You can sleep on the sofa. It folds right out. [Grunting, Yelling] Oh, wait. That was the old sofa. [Screeching]
jokes.setup: Homer offers Krusty the convertible sofa
jokes.punchline: Sofa apparently malfunctions or attacks, then Homer remembers it was replaced
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with Homer's delayed realization

jokes.text: But you've gotta comeback, Mel! We're a team. No, Krusty. You always treated me rather shabbily. On our last show, you poured liquid nitrogen down my pants and cracked my buttocks with a hammer.
jokes.setup: Krusty pleads with Mel to return as sidekick
jokes.punchline: Mel lists specific, extreme abuse from their partnership
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character work - reveals Krusty's cruelty with absurd specificity

jokes.text: Here's your taco, mister. Oops. It fell in the fryer. I'll get it out. Ow! Ow! Ow! Ow! Sorry, Krusty. I like it here. Mr. Johannson treats me with dignity.
jokes.setup: Mel claims his new boss treats him with dignity
jokes.punchline: Immediately burns himself retrieving food from fryer
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - claims dignity while performing degrading/dangerous task

jokes.text: Look, kids! A horsie! And what are you making, Sideshow Luke Perry? A 19th-century carousel. [Yells] Upstage me, will ya? Get the hell out!
jokes.setup: Krusty makes simple balloon animal while Luke Perry creates elaborate art
jokes.punchline: Krusty's jealous rage at being artistically outdone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty ego and insecurity - can't handle being shown up artistically

jokes.text: Look, there must be something I can do on the show. Please. For Mom's sake? Well, maybe we can find something.
jokes.setup: Luke Perry begs for role on Krusty's show
jokes.punchline: Appeals to family relationship for professional favor
jokes.explanation: Decent family dynamics humor but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Would you mind changing 'em for the show? Forget you, clown. Hey, our lyrics are like our children, man. No way. Well, okay. But here, where you say 'What I got, you gotta get and put it in you,' how about just, 'What I'd like is I'd like to hug and kiss you'? Wow! That's much better. Everyone can enjoy that.
jokes.setup: Band refuses to change lyrics, calling them sacred
jokes.punchline: Immediately loves sanitized version better than original
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of artistic integrity vs. commercial pressure with perfect reversal

jokes.text: I love you, Krusty. Quiet!
jokes.setup: Emotional fan declares love during performance
jokes.punchline: Krusty's harsh dismissal of genuine affection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty moment - cruel to fans despite needing them

jokes.text: Are you comfortable in there, Luke Perry? Fire away, Krusty. [Yelling] Aah! My face! My valuable face!
jokes.setup: Krusty prepares to shoot Luke Perry with cannon
jokes.punchline: Luke Perry's concern for his face as his primary asset
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity vanity joke with Luke Perry's looks-based career

jokes.text: Actually, I thought I'd lift this 1987 Buick Skylark over my head. Hi-yo! Johnny, that's amazing. Oh, yeah? Get a load of this. [Singing Opera]
jokes.setup: Johnny Carson claims he'll perform impossible feat of strength
jokes.punchline: Escalates to Bette Midler singing opera as competitive response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd celebrity talent competition

jokes.text: I've got to fire that agent.
jokes.setup: After demonstrating impossible physical and vocal feats
jokes.punchline: Carson's dry complaint about his representation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carson-style deadpan delivery and showbiz complaint

jokes.text: This was a great show, Krusty. You deserve an Emmy for this. Forget it. The Academy hates me. I don't know why.
jokes.setup: Lisa praises Krusty's comeback special
jokes.punchline: Krusty claims persecution by Emmy voters
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Krusty's persecution complex

jokes.text: Here's that ruby-studded clown nose you ordered, Krusty. [Sobbing] Oy!
jokes.setup: Expensive luxury item delivered to newly successful Krusty
jokes.punchline: Krusty's emotional reaction to frivolous purchase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty moment - immediately back to wasteful spending despite recent poverty

jokes.text: Hey, you can't come in here dressed like that! Get with the times, Moe. Yeah. I say if it feels good, do it. All right. [Chuckling] Don't snap my undies.
jokes.setup: Moe objects to customers' casual dress
jokes.punchline: Quickly adapts to new permissive attitude with personal boundary
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character work - adaptable but maintains some dignity

jokes.text: That's all right, Krusty. We're getting 50% of the T-shirt sales. What? That's the sweetest plum! You little-- Aw, what the hell. You deserve it. Thanks, kids.
jokes.setup: Bart reveals kids' business deal with Krusty
jokes.punchline: Krusty's anger transforms to grudging respect for their business savvy
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Krusty's respect for business acumen

jokes.text: To Krusty, the greatest entertainer in the world-- Except maybe that guy.
jokes.setup: Homer makes grand toast to Krusty
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts it by noticing superior performer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - well-meaning but instantly distracted and tactless
